metadata.title: Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for use of Drone Application with Pesticides for Crop Protection and for Spraying Soil & Crop Nutrients in Agricultural, Forestry, Non-Cropped Areas, etc.
metadata.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
metadata.department: Department of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
sections: Section-A: SOPs for applying pesticides using drones
Section-B: SOPs for spraying soil & crop nutrients using drones

section_A.title: SOP for Applying Pesticides for Crop Protection by Using Drones

statutory_provisions.relevant_rule: Chapter VIII, Rule 43 - Aerial Spraying Operations
statutory_provisions.UAS_rules: Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) Rules, 2021 (G.S.R. 589(E) dated 25th August, 2021)
RPAS_rules: Civil Aviation Requirements (CAR) under Rule 15A and Rule 133A of the Aircraft Rules, 1937 under the Aircraft Act, 1934
governing_bodies: Central Insecticides Board and Registration Committee (CIB&RC)
aerial_spraying_operator_obligations: Marking of the area shall be the responsibility of the operators
Operators shall use only approved insecticides and their formulations at approved concentration and height
Washing decontamination and first-aid facilities shall be provided by the operators

All aerial operations shall be notified to the public not less than 24 hours in advance through competent authorities (Executive Officer of respective Gram Panchayat & Panchayat Samiti, and concerned Agriculture Officer)
Animals and persons not connected with the operations shall be prevented from entering such areas for a specific period
Pilots shall undergo specialization training including clinical effects of the insecticides
statutory_provisions.note: Operators have flexibility to utilize any RPAS/drone for agricultural pesticide spraying irrespective of weight category or use case, provided the discharge of substance is cleared and mentioned in the UAOP issued by DGCA

pre_application_checklist: Confirm not to fly in drone-forbidden area (airport or electronic station). No prior permission required for operating in green zone
Ensure drone is Digital Sky Compliant with 'No Permission – No Take-off' hardware and firmware
Obtain Unique Identification Number (UIN) from DGCA for operating in controlled airspace and affix it on drone
Obtain Unmanned Aircraft Operator Permit (UAOP) if applicable from DGCA for commercial operations
Ensure drone is in good condition (not damaged) and fit for flying safely
Keep an eye on interference from mobile devices or signal blockage
Fly in visual line of sight (VLOS): Always be within visual range of drone
Ensure operators are trained on both drone operation and safe use of pesticide
No alcoholic drinks should be consumed within 8 hours preceding operation
Calibrate drone spray system to ensure nozzle output and accurate application of labelled rates
Check if drone is in good condition and that there is no leak in the spraying system
Confirm a place for take-off, landing and tank mix operations
Check and mark proposed treated area, boundary, and obstacles (walls, trees) around the field for safe operation
Set up the least buffer zone (as specified by DGCA/CIB&RC) between drone treatment and the non-target crop
Confirm presence of water sources - Do not spray pesticides within 100m from water sources to avoid polluting water
Check if the public has been notified at least 24 hours in advance through written intimation to competent authorities
Ensure animals and persons not connected with operations are prevented from entering for a specific period
Log flights and intimate concerned authorities (DGCA, local police) of any incidents/accidents
Don't fly drone over groups of people, public events, or stadiums without permission
Don't fly drone over government facilities/military bases or near any no-drone zones
Don't fly drone over private property unless permission is given
Don't fly drone in controlled airspace near airports without filing flight plan or AAI/ADC permission (at least 24 hours before)
Don't drop or carry hazardous material
Don't fly drone from a moving vehicle, ship or aircraft
Don't fly drone in contravention to UAS Rules, 2021 (G.S.R. 589(E) dated 25th August, 2021)
during_application_checklist: Read labels carefully to understand safety guidance
Do not eat, drink or smoke while spraying
Confirm the flying route was reasonable to minimize turn around
Operation team shall always stay at the downwind end of the field and backlight direction
Spray with pure water first to test operation for at least 5 minutes wherever feasible
Ensure two-step dilutions to fully dissolve the pesticide
Adopt proper pressure for optimized droplet spectrum (>100µm)
Check weather conditions for: Appropriate Wind speed, Appropriate Temperature, Appropriate Humidity
Ensure appropriate flying height above target crop
Avoid walking through crop contaminated by drifting spray
Do not spray during active bee foraging period of the day. Avoid spray drift to flowering nectar crop
When spraying pesticides toxic to non-target organisms (fish, birds, silkworm), strictly abide by product label requirements
Use anti-drift nozzle to decrease drift to human and environment
post_application_checklist: Timely evacuation and transfer to fresh air
Triple rinse of empty container is mandatory
Ensure waste generated is kept to a minimum
The disposal of waste must conform to the local laws
Never burn or bury hazardous waste
Never leave empty containers in the field. Dispose as per Insecticides Rule 1971
Set up warning signs in the spray area for reminding people
Take a shower and put on clean clothes
To prevent leakage of plant protection products in the process of transport and waiting to use
Securely store plant protection products away from unauthorized people, animals and food. Safely dispose all spills immediately
Follow the maintenance schedule as prescribed by the Drone manufacturer

drone_related: Only DGCA certified/approved drone shall be permitted to carry-out agriculture spray

Drone must have capability to handle variable payload (depleting tank); nozzle system should ensure continuous spray swath from minimum permitted height above uniformly distributed crop (e.g. paddy/sugarcane)
Drone must be fitted with accurate altitude sensor to maintain desired height throughout the spraying mission
GPS accuracy of drone and map shall be characterized and utilized to define safety/buffer margin while creating geo-fencing around the field or obstacles
Drone spray system must support variable flow control to ensure uniform dispensing of payload
Drone must have necessary fail-safes including Return to Home (RTH) on empty tank and auto mission restart from the point RTH was engaged
Drone spray system should be leak proof; dripping of Pesticides/Insecticides should be avoided during application (check before flight)
pesticides_insecticides: Only CIB&RC approved Pesticides/Insecticides shall be used
Dose must remain within the range approved by CIB&RC

Pesticides/Insecticides (liquid/solid) compatibility with drone spray system shall be established prior to mission for desired dilution - to ensure solubility, formulation stability and ability to spray with nozzle type provided
In case of mixing more than one Pesticide/Insecticide, CIB&RC specified guidelines must be adhered to
Minimum dilution shall be decided based on the above requirements ensuring satisfactory coverage both horizontally and vertically
Pesticides/Insecticides should be diluted only with clean water or other suitable ingredient as approved by CIB&RC
environment_limitations: Drone based spray permitted only under conducive weather conditions
Key environmental parameters: Appropriate Wind speed, Temperature, and Relative Humidity
pilot_training: Only DGCA certified pilots shall be permitted to fly agri drones

Training module as devised by NIPHM, Hyderabad encompassing pesticide/insecticide handling, agri-mission specific operational protocols, and relevant crop protection guidelines shall be mandatory for pilots operating pesticide/insecticide spray drones

drift_management.description: To minimize spray drift, apart from wind limitation, the following are important
parameters: A buffer zone can be demarcated (geo fenced)
Spray schedule should be at appropriate time gap either before or after rainfall
Others, as specified in guidelines issued by CIB&RC from time to time

safeguarding_non_targets: Buffer zone as per CIB&RC approved guidelines shall be maintained between adjacent farms or different crops to avoid spray on non-targets
During spray operation, operator should maintain the distance approved by CIB&RC from the drone and avoid windward direction
No human or animal movement shall be permitted within the farm during and immediately after spray operations
Drone based pesticide spray operations should be conducted at a distance approved by CIB&RC from water bodies, residential areas, fodder crops, public utilities, dairy, poultry etc.

pesticide_registration.authority: Central Insecticides Board and Registration Committee (CIB&RC)
pesticide_registration.note: Registration requirements are dynamic and will be considered based on safety, efficacy, statutory and legal requirements and published by CIB&RC from time to time
pesticide_registration.application_process: Applicant shall apply before Secretariat of CIB&RC, in the manner prescribed by CIB&RC under the Insecticides Act, 1968

spray_monitoring_and_data_submission.submission_method: Email (as mentioned in CIB&RC guidelines) or online portal created for the purpose

section_3_pesticide_data: Is the insecticide mixed with water or solvent? (Y/N)
If yes, what solvent and mixing ratio
section_5_spray_application: ROTARY SPRAYERS: speed setting (blade angle, pulley setting, no. batteries)
Flow rate setting (which nozzle or restrictor used)
AERIAL SPRAYING: support supplied (GP=ground party/RC=radio communication/TG=DGPS track guidance)
section_6_control_efficacy: Yield of Crop/method of Effectiveness/mortality estimation (Quadrats/Target size/Visual/Cages/Other) - QTVCO
section_7_safety_and_environment: Protective clothing worn by operator (Goggles/Mask/gloves/Overalls/Boots) - GMLOB
Who was informed of spraying? (Farmers/Nomads/Villagers/Officials/Beekeeper/others) - FNVOB
If yes, what type of effect?
Details of anyone who felt unwell or if other problems were encountered
Details about nearby water-bodies and water channels

section_B.title: SOP for Spraying Soil & Crop Nutrients by Using Drones

nutrient_assessment_and_mapping: Soil nutrient mapping can be helpful for real-time site-specific nutrients management
Traditional methods give properties of soil at sampled locations only; UAV provides properties of entire field in short time
UAV based soil nutrient analysis is less time-consuming and energy efficient; enables precise timely application of deficient nutrients
Drones fly over field and take high resolution pictures sent to cloud/software facilitating prescription maps
Maps can be uploaded on farm equipment to precisely regulate delivery of inputs (fertilizers)

considerations: Form/concentration of the nutrient being sprayed/broadcasted
Sensors/spraying systems installed on the drone
spraying_of_soil_crop_nutrients.efficiency_vs_knapsack: At least 10 times more area can be sprayed per day per drone compared to traditional knapsack sprayers

drone_sensor_arrangements: Drone with fertilizer spraying system for granules/liquid
Drone with multispectral camera and spraying system for granules/liquid

nutrient_delivery_protocols.protocol: RDF Based Protocol (Recommended Dose of Fertilizer)
nutrient_delivery_protocols.description: Nutrient application done based on recommendations at each crop stage and soil nutrient requirement. Can be immediately deployed for liquid and granular nutrients (Macro, Micro and Nano-Micro/Macro fertilizers)

nutrient_delivery_protocols.step_1: Soil imaging of the field to assess soil nutrient status; post-process data to generate GPS-tagged precision nutrient requirements map using soil indices; software calculates amounts of primary, secondary and micro nutrients for each crop stage
nutrient_delivery_protocols.step_2: Drone with fertilizer spraying/dispenser system applies fertilizer by controlling fertilizer metering unit to achieve variable rate application
nutrient_delivery_protocols.status: Can be standardized in near future

nutrient_delivery_protocols.description: Imaging and spraying/dispensing of nutrients placed on drone; imaging, live processing and delivery happen simultaneously in real-time. Requires powerful live-processing hardware and software to process multispectral camera data without lag
nutrient_delivery_protocols.status: Needs more R&D for practical feasibility

camera_and_sensor_recommendations: Multispectral (Coarse bands) and Hyper spectral (Fine bands) can be used for soil analysis using Geo and Time tagged images
AI and ML algorithms can be used to develop models for soil analysis and identify issues
LiDAR sensor may be used to capture data in dense canopy crop
Electrostatic nozzle may be used to avoid drift during foliar application
foliar_application_notes: Foliar application is recommended to start with as soil database development is time consuming
Application of nutrient or urea in foliar form uniformly can be started directly without waiting for R&D on variable mapping
For variable application, real-time mapping using NDVI or other VIs can be done for prediction of nutrient deficiency
For foliar application, spraying system may be fitted with rotary atomization nozzles to reduce drift
Foliar application especially suitable for plantation crops (coffee), hilly terrain crops (apples), and flat land crops (sugarcane)

feasibility_of_sustainable_drone_adoptio: Drones are available at affordable prices and capable of imaging ground data with corresponding geographic locations
UAVs are autonomous drones that can be programmed to operate as per a defined plan for precision data collection and input delivery
Drones provide a quick image of a field in a fraction of time and cover maximum land in a single flight (synoptic view/assessment)
Drone industry is rapidly increasing in India in several sectors including agriculture
Micronutrient powder application on cabbage crop can be done with 8-10 litre water to minimize phyto-toxicity
Drones can be used for spraying nutrients after diluting in water (10 litres) and also to broadcast fine granules up to 10 kg per acre
Mission Planner Software uses Google Maps for flight planning and Satellite based GPS for precise drone navigation
Bio liquid fertilizers can be allowed to spray on different crops using drone; chemical liquid fertilizers formulation has to be checked through experimentation
For variable application, real time mapping of nutrients using NDVI or other suitable VIs can be done
Rate of flow and amount of soil nutrients to be sprayed through each nozzle is configurable electronically through Ground Control Station (GCS)
Different nozzle types (Centrifugal, Electrostatic, Atomizer, etc.) can be configured based on type of crop and nutrients
A drone of 10 kg payload capacity in normal conditions can cover 30 acres per day (6 hours/day) with 5 sets of batteries

storage_procedures_for_FPOs_CHCs_service: Maintain a written inventory of all materials
Follow crop nutrient manufacturer's recommendation for storage
Do not store fertilizers in damp or dirty places; ensure bags do not absorb moisture from leaky roofs or water seepage
Store in a secured building to prevent unauthorized access
Keep crop nutrient materials away from hazardous materials like herbicides, pesticides etc.
Keep materials as far away as possible from doors or places where spills could reach outside
Regularly inspect storage area for spills and leaks
Provide emergency eyewash and emergency drench showers within or near the storage area
Dispose of waste, excess or obsolete materials and chemicals in accordance with manufacturer's recommendation and state law
Improper mixing and storage of fertilizers can result in large nutrient losses
Provide absorbent spill kits in all liquid storage areas

general_rules: FCO guidelines to be followed for use of all crop nutrients in agriculture
Crop nutrients should be applied by properly trained personnel
good_agricultural_application_practices: Type and amount of crop nutrients to be used as per recommendation of approved agencies or institutions
Dose and compatibility of the nutrients should be checked before spraying
Fertilize when the soil is wet (good agricultural practices provided separately)
Never apply crop nutrients when heavy rain is anticipated
Calibrate crop nutrient application (distribution, spraying, and broadcast) system regularly to ensure proper application and loading rates
Mix crop nutrients in clean application equipment under cover in an area where spills will not come into contact with rainwater or storm-water runoff
The excess use of crop and soil nutrients and mixing with pesticides may cause phytotoxicity; in this case, guidelines for pesticide application may be referred

procedures_for_liquid_nutrient_applicati.nozzle_placement: Preferably keep nozzle beneath the motors to minimize drift of droplets
procedures_for_liquid_nutrient_applicati.note: Restrict the spraying to the intended area

drone_servicing_and_operation: Stable and safe drone designs like hexacopter are recommended for spraying of crop nutrient
Follow the user manual for calibration and maintenance of the drone before and after operations as recommended by the manufacturer
Follow DGCA guidelines and Dr. Alagusundaram Committee's recommendations for operation of the agricultural 'Krishi' drone

drone_components_to_check: Real time kinematic global positioning system (RTK GPS)
operator_requirements: Service provider and operator should have flying permissions and flying license from DGCA
Operator must have training certificate on operation, repair and maintenance and safe use of soil and crop nutrient from ICAR/Govt. approved training centers
Operator should be healthy, of sound mind and steady during operation

roles: One or two technicians for preparation of formulation and mixing and troubleshooting

weather_restrictions: Do not operate during cloudy and rainy conditions
Avoid operation immediately before and after rains
Avoid operation in opposite wind direction
field_safety_requirements: Crop and field conditions and obstacles near the field should be monitored
Home point of drone should be selected keeping in view the crop and field conditions
Flying path should be prepared according to field shape and required head space for turning
Safe distance not less than 10m of nearby people from drone take off place must be ensured
Operation team shall always stay at the downwind end of the field and backlight direction
Avoid flying of the drones near high-tension electrical towers to avoid signal interferences
bee_and_non_target_organism_protection: Reach of soil and crop nutrient spray should be avoided during active bee foraging period in flowering nectar crop
When spraying nutrients toxic to non-target organisms (fish, birds, silkworm), strictly abide by product label requirements

checks_required: nozzle wear out for uniform spraying
calibration_and_equipment_check.water_requirement: Always use clean water for preparing the solution of soil and crop nutrient
calibration_and_equipment_check.container_rule: Containers and buckets used for mixing soil and crop nutrient should never be used for domestic purpose even after thorough washing

during_application_standards_for_nutrien: Monitor drone behavior continuously during operation to avoid accidents; spraying system should also be monitored for not being choked during operation
Drone should be flown at a height of 1.0 to 3.0m above the targeted crop canopy at a speed not exceeding 8 m/s during spraying
Discharged drone batteries should be replaced immediately
Never apply over-dose and higher concentration of crop nutrients than the recommended
Movement of human or animal should not be allowed in the operational field during spraying using drones
post_application_standards_for_nutrients: After completion of spraying, operator and team should leave the operational site to receive fresh air. Entry to the treated field immediately after operation should be avoided without wearing protective clothing
Warning sign at least for 2 hours may be put at the sprayed site to avoid hazards to humans
Left over spray solutions should be disposed-off at safer place like barren and isolated areas. Triple rinse of empty containers should be done
Disposal of waste must conform to the local laws. Hazardous wastes should never be burnt or buried. Empty containers should never be left in the field
Empty containers of crop nutrients should not be re-used for storing other articles
While transporting or storing PPPs (Plant Protection Products), the area should be beyond the reach of children, animals and unauthorized persons

Hands and face need to be washed with clean water and preferably with soap immediately after the spraying operation. Clothes must be changed. In case any sign of body contamination with hazardous chemicals is perceived, consult a doctor
After every 20 hours of flight, the drone should be inspected for wear, loosen screws, scratches and distortion in propeller and frames
